name: NIST CSF
description: NIST Cybersecurity Framework version 2.0 — a set of guidelines for mitigating organizational cybersecurity risks across 6 functions and 23 categories with 106 controls.

families.description: The Govern (GV) function encompasses organizational, management, and technical activities to enable an organization to establish systems and processes that are foundational to protecting the organization and its stakeholders.

controls.title: Organizational context is understood
controls.description: The organization understands its organizational context and how it creates, processes, maintains, and exchanges information and data that could be affected by or could affect cybersecurity.

controls.title: External environment is understood
controls.description: The organization understands the external environment in which it operates and how cybersecurity risks in that environment can affect the organization.

controls.title: Organizational direction is established
controls.description: The organization establishes organizational direction and commitment regarding cybersecurity through communication of organizational values, mission, vision, and the role of cybersecurity.

controls.title: Cybersecurity accountability is established
controls.description: The organization establishes roles and responsibilities for cybersecurity, including roles and responsibilities for the governance of cybersecurity.

controls.title: Risk management strategy is established
controls.description: The organization establishes and implements a risk management strategy that includes determining risk tolerance and the organization's approach to risk assessment, analysis, response, and monitoring.

controls.title: Organizational risk is determined
controls.description: The organization determines organizational risk by analyzing the likelihood and magnitude of negative impacts from cybersecurity events.

controls.title: Risk appetite is established
controls.description: The organization establishes a risk appetite that is used to guide decisions about risk response and the organization's tolerance for risk.

controls.title: Rules of engagement are established
controls.description: The organization establishes rules of engagement for cybersecurity interactions and engagements with internal and external stakeholders.

controls.title: Agreements with external entities are established
controls.description: The organization establishes agreements with external entities that include terms for the secure exchange and storage of information and data.

controls.title: Supply chain risk management is conducted
controls.description: The organization conducts supply chain risk management activities with the intent to manage the cybersecurity risks associated with the development, procurement, maintenance, and disposal of organization information, systems, and services.

controls.title: Policies are established
controls.description: The organization establishes and implements cybersecurity policies, processes, procedures, and standards that include the identification of information and systems to be protected, roles and responsibilities, and enforcement mechanisms.

controls.title: Cybersecurity is integrated into planning processes
controls.description: The organization integrates cybersecurity into organizational planning processes and programs, including business continuity planning, operational planning, capital planning, and security planning.

controls.title: Policies are monitored
controls.description: The organization monitors and reviews its cybersecurity policies and procedures on a periodic basis to determine their effectiveness and to ensure alignment with organizational objectives.

controls.title: Cybersecurity status is visible
controls.description: The organization provides cybersecurity information and the current status of cybersecurity risk at all levels of the organization through appropriate communication mechanisms.

controls.title: Environmental factors are understood
controls.description: The organization understands environmental factors relevant to its cybersecurity program including regulatory factors, legal factors, and other requirements related to cybersecurity.

controls.title: Cybersecurity supply chain risk management is integrated
controls.description: The organization integrates cybersecurity supply chain risk management into the organization's overall cybersecurity program.

controls.title: Cyber supply chain risk management process is established
controls.description: The organization establishes a cyber supply chain risk management process and assigns roles and responsibilities for managing cyber supply chain risks.

controls.title: Supply chain relationships are reviewed
controls.description: The organization reviews the cybersecurity risks associated with organizational suppliers and business partners, including the security capabilities of external providers and their ability to meet security requirements.

controls.title: Supply chain events are managed
controls.description: The organization manages cybersecurity events or conditions that may result from upstream or downstream supply chain partners.

families.description: The Identify (ID) function encompasses activities to develop an organizational understanding to manage cybersecurity risk to systems, people, assets, data, and capabilities.

controls.title: Physical devices and systems within the organization are inventoried
controls.description: Physical devices and systems within the organization are inventoried and details such as device type, model, serial number, function, location, and network address are recorded.

controls.title: Software platforms and applications within the organization are inventoried
controls.description: Software platforms and applications within the organization are inventoried and details such as software/application name, version, type, vendor information, hosting location, and license information are recorded.

controls.title: Organizational communication and data flows are mapped
controls.description: Communication and data flows (including user identification, purpose, and authorization levels) throughout the organization are mapped and documented.

controls.title: Asset vulnerabilities are identified and documented
controls.description: Asset vulnerabilities are identified, documented, categorized, and communicated to organizational personnel so that the vulnerabilities can be addressed.

controls.title: Threat and vulnerability information is received from external organizations
controls.description: Threat and vulnerability information is continuously received from external organizations and used to inform organizational risk assessments.

controls.title: Internal threats are identified and documented
controls.description: Internal threats are identified and documented. As part of the threat identification process, the organization determines potential internal threats and evaluates the likelihood and magnitude of negative impacts.

controls.title: Organizational data is inventoried
controls.description: Organizational data, including categorization, is inventoried based on the results of the information and asset risk assessment process.

controls.title: Data handling practices are established
controls.description: Data handling practices are established in accordance with the organization's risk strategy to include the classification, labeling, handling, retention, and secure disposal of data.

controls.title: Data is securely disposed of
controls.description: Data is securely disposed of in accordance with the organization's data security and privacy policies, and its information risk management strategy.

families.description: The Protect (PR) function encompasses activities to develop and implement appropriate safeguards to ensure delivery of critical services.

controls.title: Identities and credentials are managed for authorized devices, users and processes
controls.description: Identities and credentials are issued, managed, verified, revoked, and audited for authorized devices, users, and processes to ensure that only authorized access is allowed.

controls.title: Access is managed for organizational assets
controls.description: Access to organizational assets is managed through physical and logical controls in accordance with roles and responsibilities.

controls.title: Personnel are provided cybersecurity awareness and training
controls.description: Personnel are provided cybersecurity awareness training and regular updates on changes to threats, vulnerabilities, and organizational responses to maintain awareness and skills.

controls.title: Specialized training is provided for personnel
controls.description: Specialized training is provided to personnel who manage, operate, or maintain organizational assets on the security of those assets.

controls.title: Data-at-rest is protected
controls.description: Data-at-rest is protected with appropriate safeguards, such as encryption, to address the organization's risk strategy.

controls.title: Data-in-transit is protected
controls.description: Data-in-transit is protected with appropriate safeguards, such as encryption, during transmission over external networks to address the organization's risk strategy.

controls.title: Media containing organizational information is protected
controls.description: Media containing organizational information is protected and disposed of in accordance with the organization's risk strategy.

controls.title: Adequate capacity to ensure availability is maintained
controls.description: Adequate capacity to ensure availability is maintained to address the organization's risk strategy.

controls.title: Processes and procedures are established and implemented
controls.description: Processes and procedures are established and implemented to manage the physical environment such that organizational assets are protected from environmental hazards and unauthorized access.

controls.title: System redundancy is available to support system resiliency
controls.description: System redundancy is available to support organizational resilience in the event of a component failure or cyber attack.

controls.title: Information systems and associated information are protected
controls.description: Information systems and associated information are protected from unauthorized access and other adverse impacts during initial system design and development and throughout the system's lifecycle.

controls.title: Information systems development and deployment are managed
controls.description: The organization manages the development and deployment of organizational information systems and associated information through a formalized process that incorporates security considerations.

controls.title: Configuration management is performed
controls.description: Configuration management is performed to ensure that information systems are configured and maintained according to the organization's risk management strategy.

controls.title: Information systems and constituent components are managed through their operational life
controls.description: Information systems and constituent components are managed through their operational life to protect the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of organizational information.

families.description: The Detect (DE) function encompasses activities to develop and implement appropriate monitoring to detect cybersecurity events on a timely basis.

controls.title: Networks and network services are monitored to find anomalies
controls.description: Networks and network services are monitored to detect potential cybersecurity events through network traffic analysis and other monitoring techniques.

controls.title: Suspicious activity is monitored and analyzed
controls.description: Suspicious activity is monitored and analyzed by personnel or automation in accordance with the organization's risk strategy. This activity is analyzed to determine if the suspicious activity is a cybersecurity event.

controls.title: Personnel, devices, and software are monitored
controls.description: Personnel, devices, and software are monitored to ensure that cybersecurity event indicators are detected and acted upon in accordance with the organization's risk management strategy.

controls.title: Malicious code is detected
controls.description: Malicious code is detected through automated tools or scanning mechanisms and is managed in accordance with the organization's risk management strategy.

controls.title: Cyber threat and vulnerability information is understood
controls.description: Cyber threat intelligence and information regarding cyber vulnerabilities are understood. The organization understands cyber threats, vulnerabilities, and threat-vulnerability pairs relevant to its organizational context.

controls.title: Detection activities are coordinated with other organizations
controls.description: Detection activities are coordinated with internal and external stakeholders and threat information is shared with appropriate parties.

controls.title: Event data is analyzed to support response activities
controls.description: Event data is collected and analyzed to support response activities and to improve detection.

controls.title: Detection processes are continuously improved
controls.description: Detection processes are continuously improved through feedback and analysis of cybersecurity events.

families.description: The Respond (RS) function encompasses activities to take action regarding a detected cybersecurity event to stop the attack and mitigate its effects.

controls.title: Incidents are reported
controls.description: Incidents are reported to appropriate parties within the organization, and externally as required by law or regulation, or with other communities.

controls.title: Incident response processes are executed and managed
controls.description: Incident response processes are executed and managed to ensure response to a cybersecurity incident in a manner consistent with the incident response plan.

controls.title: Incident information is gathered and managed
controls.description: Incident information is gathered, generated, and managed to ensure that a consistent and coordinated response is achieved.

controls.title: The impact of incidents is determined
controls.description: The extent of the cybersecurity incident is determined and documented as an input to the organization's response process.

controls.title: Forensic analysis is performed
controls.description: Forensic analysis, when appropriate, is performed on organizational information and data following a cybersecurity incident.

controls.title: Containment is achieved
controls.description: The organization achieves containment of a cybersecurity incident to stop or reduce the scope and impact of the incident.

controls.title: Incident response is executed and managed
controls.description: Incident response is executed and managed to prevent expansion of the event and to mitigate its effects.

controls.title: Incident remediation is completed
controls.description: Remediation of a cybersecurity incident is completed and verified to ensure that organizational information and assets have been restored or replaced.

controls.title: Identified vulnerabilities are corrected
controls.description: Identified vulnerabilities associated with a cybersecurity incident are corrected through the normal vulnerability management process.

controls.title: Incident-related information is communicated
controls.description: Incident-related information is communicated to appropriate parties within the organization and externally as required.

families.description: The Recover (RC) function encompasses activities to restore capabilities or services that were impaired due to a cybersecurity event.

controls.title: Recovery plan is executed during or after a cybersecurity incident
controls.description: Recovery activities are executed during or after a cybersecurity incident to restore organizational operations to normal levels or as close to normal levels as possible.

controls.title: Recovery activities and progress are communicated
controls.description: Recovery activities and progress in restoring capabilities and services are communicated to appropriate parties within the organization and externally as required.

controls.title: Recovery processes are coordinated
controls.description: Recovery processes are coordinated and managed to restore organizational capabilities and services following a cybersecurity incident.

controls.title: Resilience is improved based on recovery activities
controls.description: The organization improves its resilience based on lessons learned from the incident recovery process.
